mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 200) indicates accommodative monetary policy, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 200.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through stronger corporate earnings.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are negative when both GDPC1 and INDPRO show positive growth in the prior quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 5.0) reflects tightening labor markets, which boosts consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where UNRATE remains below 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 120) indicates improving manufacturing capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 120.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 2000) signal increased construction activity, which boosts related sectors and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where HOUST exceeds 2000.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through multiple sectors.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative when both RSAFS and HOUST show positive growth in the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0) signals monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in 60%+ of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0.
